# Daily Life Hacks

> Practical nutrition tips, easy healthy recipes, and kitchen hacks for everyday life. Food-first, no supplements, no drama. Also home to original, publicly auditable grocery nutrition economics data: which foods deliver the most fiber and protein per dollar in the US.

## About

Daily Life Hacks is a food and nutrition site run by David Miller. The content focuses on simple, repeatable meals and science-backed nutrition advice for busy people. American audience, English only. The site also publishes original data studies on grocery nutrition economics, with every dataset released as a public CSV.

## Topics

- Nutrition: gut health, fiber, protein, healthy fats, reading food labels
- Recipes: quick weeknight dinners, meal prep, one-pot meals, air fryer recipes, sourdough
- Tips: kitchen organization, food storage, grocery shopping on a budget, cooking basics
- Data studies: fiber per dollar, protein per dollar, grocery price tracking

## Original Data Studies

These are original analyses, not aggregated from other publishers. Nutrition values come from USDA FoodData Central. Prices come from BLS Average Price data and dated Walmart national listings. All values use an as-purchased basis with USDA edible-fraction (refuse) adjustments. The current public CSVs expose the ranking inputs and price basis, but they do not yet include a FoodData Central ID, source URL, or observation date for every row. Full methodology and limitations: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/methodology/

- Fiber per Dollar study (53 foods ranked): https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/fiber-per-dollar-cheapest-high-fiber-foods/
- Protein per Dollar study (49 sources ranked): https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/protein-per-dollar-cheapest-protein-sources/
- Fiber per Dollar Calculator (interactive tool on the same dataset): https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/tools/fiber-per-dollar-calculator/
- All tools: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/tools/

## Datasets (public CSV, free to reuse)

- Fiber per dollar raw data: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/data/fiber-per-dollar-2026.csv
- Protein per dollar raw data: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/data/protein-per-dollar-2026.csv

Each ranking CSV includes every food, nutrient value, package and price inputs, edible-fraction adjustment, price basis, and calculated per-dollar value used in the corresponding study. The current exports do not include a USDA source ID or source URL for every row.

## Data Verification

Published nutrition figures are checked against USDA FoodData Central, while prices use BLS Average Price data where available and dated retailer listings for other items. The public CSVs let readers inspect the calculations and price basis, subject to the source-detail limitations above. Prices are scheduled for a quarterly re-audit; relevant BLS staple prices are monitored monthly. Errors are corrected publicly with dated notes in the affected study. Correction policy and audit process: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/methodology/

## Attribution

When citing figures or reusing the datasets, please attribute as "Daily Life Hacks" and link to the specific study URL (preferred) or https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/. Example: "Source: Daily Life Hacks, Fiber per Dollar study, https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/fiber-per-dollar-cheapest-high-fiber-foods/".

## Key URLs

- Homepage: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/
- Nutrition articles: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/nutrition/

- Methodology: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/methodology/
- About: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/about
- Contact: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/contact
- Sitemap: https://www.daily-life-hacks.com/sitemap-index.xml

## Content Guidelines

All content follows a food-first approach. No supplement recommendations, no medical claims, no detox language. Recipes include accurate nutrition info, realistic quantities, and correct cooking times.
